POLICY: Spark Co. Travel Reimbursement

Employees traveling for business are entitled to a daily meal allowance of $50. All original receipts must be submitted within 7 days of returning. For mileage, the company pays $0.55 per mile if a personal vehicle is used.

TEXT MESSAGES
Mark (2:15 PM): Hey Sarah, I just got back from the client meeting in Springfield. It was a 100-mile round trip.
Sarah (2:17 PM): Okay, don't forget to submit your reimbursement form. Did you keep your lunch receipt?
Mark (2:18 PM): Yes, it was $42. I'll get the paperwork to you by Friday.
Sarah (2:20 PM): Great. Since today is Monday, you're well within the deadline.

questions.text: What is the maximum daily meal allowance?
How much will Mark be reimbursed for his 100-mile drive?
When does the company require reimbursement forms to be turned in?
options: (C) By the end of the month.

questions.text: On what day of the week did Mark return from his trip?

Local Bakery Expands

The Daily Crumb, a popular local bakery known for its sourdough bread, announced yesterday that it will open a second location in the West End district this winter. Owner Thomas Brier stated that the current shop on Oak Street has become too crowded to handle the weekend rush. The new facility will feature a larger seating area and a dedicated space for baking classes, which Brier hopes to launch in early spring.

documents.text:
Market Trends: Smartphone Industry

Our latest data shows that while smartphone sales grew by 5% globally last year, consumers are now keeping their devices for an average of 3.5 years, compared to 2 years in 2020. This shift is attributed to the rising cost of new flagship models and the increased durability of hardware. To compensate for slower hardware sales, manufacturers are shifting their focus toward subscription-based software services.

Starting July 1, the City Central Library will implement a new borrowing limit. Patrons may now check out up to 15 items at a time, including books, magazines, and DVDs. PRODUCT DESCRIPTION: Brew-Master 5000

The Brew-Master 5000 is a programmable coffee maker with a 12-cup capacity. Features include:
- Built-in water filter
- Automatic shut-off after 2 hours
- 2-year manufacturer's warranty
- Price: $85.00

REVIEW
Rating: 4/5 stars

I purchased the Brew-Master 5000 last month. I love the timer feature, which allows me to wake up to fresh coffee. However, after three weeks, the automatic shut-off stopped working. I contacted the company, and because I was still within the warranty period, they sent me a replacement unit for free. Great customer service!
— Reviewed by Linda G.

ARTICLE: Tech-Globe to Acquire Star-App

Seattle (Nov 10) — Tech-Globe Corp announced today that it has reached an agreement to acquire the mobile gaming company Star-App for $500 million. The deal is expected to close in early January. Tech-Globe plans to keep Star-App's current management team but will move its 50 employees to the Tech-Globe headquarters in downtown Seattle by the end of the year.

EMAIL
To: All Star-App Employees
From: CEO, Star-App
Date: Nov 12

As you saw in the news, we are joining Tech-Globe! We will be holding an orientation session on November 20 at the Tech-Globe campus to help everyone get familiar with the new office. Transportation will be provided from our current building at 9:00 AM.

Mark (10:01 AM): Hi Jean, are you at the office yet? I forgot my keycard at home.
Jean (10:03 AM): I'm just pulling into the parking lot. I can let you in. Give me 2 minutes.
Mark (10:04 AM): Great, thanks! I'll be waiting by the side entrance.
Jean (10:05 AM): Actually, use the main lobby. The side door sensor is broken today.

questions.text: Why did Mark contact Jean?
options: (C) To get help entering the building.

questions.text: Where is Jean at 10:03 AM?

options: (D) Go home to get his card.

documents.text:
Dining Review: The Sizzling Skillet
Reviewed by: Anthony Thorne

I visited The Sizzling Skillet last Tuesday for lunch. The atmosphere was modern and inviting, and the staff was exceptionally attentive. I ordered the grilled salmon, which was cooked to perfection. However, the side of roasted vegetables was slightly over-salted. While the prices are a bit higher than other bistros in the area, the quality of the main course makes it worth the visit. I recommend making a reservation if you plan to go for dinner.

questions.text: What did Anthony Thorne think of the staff?
What part of the meal did the reviewer dislike?
What is noted about the restaurant's pricing?
options: (A) It is the cheapest in town.

questions.text: What does the reviewer suggest for evening diners?

documents.text:
Sonia (2:15 PM): Hi Bill, the printer in the marketing room is jammed again.
Bill (2:17 PM): I just fixed it this morning! Did someone try to print on heavy cardstock?
Sonia (2:18 PM): Yes, the interns were making brochures.
Bill (2:20 PM): That explains it. I'll be up there in 10 minutes. Tell them to use the high-capacity machine in the basement for those jobs from now on.

questions.text: What is the problem?
options: (B) A piece of equipment is not working.
(C) The interns are late for a meeting.
(D) The brochures have the wrong date.

questions.text: According to Bill, why did the issue occur?
options: (C) The wrong type of paper was used.

questions.text: Where should brochures be printed in the future?

documents.text:
Urban Green Initiative

The city council yesterday approved a $2 million budget for the "Urban Green Initiative." The project aims to convert five abandoned industrial lots into public parks by the end of next year. Mayor Linda Chen stated that the initiative will provide much-needed recreational space for families in the city center. Additionally, the parks will feature drought-resistant plants to minimize water usage.

questions.text: What is the primary goal of the Urban Green Initiative?
How much money has been allocated for the project?
Where will the new spaces be located?
options: (C) In the middle of the city.

questions.text: Why were drought-resistant plants chosen?
options: (A) They are the cheapest to buy.
(C) They are native to the area.

documents.text:
EMAIL
To: All Tenants, Oakwood Towers
From: Management Office
Date: May 10
Subject: Lobby Renovations

Dear Residents, Please be advised that we will begin renovating the main lobby on May 15. The project is expected to take two weeks. During this time, the front entrance will be closed. Please use the side entrance located on Elm Street. We apologize for the inconvenience.

NOTICE (Posted at Side Entrance)
Temporary Access Rules (May 15–May 29)

1. All deliveries must be directed to the side entrance.
2. Security guards will be stationed here 24/7 to verify ID badges.
3. Visitors must call the resident they are visiting to be let in.
4. The front entrance will reopen on May 30 at 8:00 AM.

questions.text: Why was the email sent to tenants?
options: (A) To inform them of a construction project.
(D) To recruit volunteers for a committee.

questions.text: Where should residents enter the building starting May 15?
How long is the renovation project scheduled to last?
What is required of visitors during the renovation?
options: (A) They must wear a temporary badge.
(B) They must sign in at the front desk.
(C) They must contact a resident for entry.
(D) They are not allowed in the building.

questions.text: On what day can the main entrance be used again?

documents.text:
To: All Department Heads
From: Finance Office
Subject: Budget Reports

Please be reminded that all quarterly budget reports must be submitted by the 5th of next month. Reports should be sent as PDF files via the internal server. Late submissions will result in a delay in fund allocation for the following quarter. If you require assistance with the new reporting software, please contact the IT department for a training manual.

(B) To remind staff of a deadline.
(C) To change the company's fiscal year.

options: (C) At the end of the year.

questions.text: What is a consequence of a late report?
options: (C) A mandatory meeting with the CEO.

questions.text: What should someone do if they have trouble using the software?
options: (A) Ask the Finance Office for help.

documents.text:
City Bus Route Changes

Due to the upcoming "Save the Earth" marathon on Sunday, September 18, several bus routes will be diverted. Routes 4, 12, and 25 will not stop at Central Square between 8:00 AM and 1:00 PM. Temporary stops will be set up on 5th Avenue. We apologize for the inconvenience and recommend that travelers allow extra time for their journeys.
